message: Jake
Ash
Welcome, the thing isn’t here yet.
Welcome
You look unusually refreshed, don't you?
Yeah, I’ve mostly finished Meryl’s request. All that's left is for the two of them to meet at the Guggenheim Museum in a week.
Then let’s raise a toast today.
But with that injury, you might want to skip drinking.
Let’s not be such a downer, just a little.
Alright, just a little then.
Here’s a Singapore Sling.
Thanks.
Ugh.
Yeah, it feels like a weight's been lifted off my shoulders. Ah.
I feel kind of good.
There’s one more good thing. The thing has arrived.
Yay, lucky! Good things really do come in succession.
That’s true. But watch out, there might be a pitfall later.
Geez, don’t scare me like that.
I wonder if the meeting between Meryl and Cindy will go well.
It better go well, or we’re in trouble.
But if I just get them to meet, at least my job has been accomplished.
Woman's voice
Hello, can I sit next to you?
Oh, huh? Is the CF shoot over?
Jennifer
Yeah, I just got back a moment ago. I thought I’d see you here.
Well, um, you...
Ah.
Um...
I actually wanted to talk a little too.
Jennifer
Eh, really? But
Do you think that's a good story?
Uh
Hmm, I wonder.
Jennifer.
You’re not saying I’m a murderer or anything ridiculous like that, are you?
W-why would you think something like that?
Jennifer.
Well, everyone is saying that. Apparently, Tonny called me a suspect in a murder case at the party.
Who told you that?
Jennifer.
Cindy told me. She said if I heard it, not to take it to heart.
Did something happen between you and Tonny?
Jennifer.
Oh, what happened to your hand?
Ah, this is just a little...
Jennifer.
Show me.
I-it’s fine, it’s nothing pretty.
Jennifer.
No way, you’re injured and taking alcohol?
You should really stop drinking.
Eh, ah.
It's okay, it’s not a big deal.
Jennifer.
Hey, you said you wanted to talk, right?
Yeah.
Jennifer.
Then, how about we move somewhere else?
Huh?
Jennifer.
See, that’s good. I have something to talk about too.
Got it.
But where exactly?
Jennifer
Just come with me.
See
Talk
Listen
Think
Move
System
The surroundings
Eva
Guy
Work
About
Eva
Guy
Work
To another place
Save
End the game
There are only a few couples besides me. It's nice that it's quiet, but if the place goes under, it'll mean one less spot to relax.
The inside of the bar, with no windows, is illuminated only by purple-red lights. It feels a bit in bad taste, but I guess I can put up with it.
On the counter, polished glasses are lined up. Keeping delicate and fragile cocktail glasses looking nice must be quite the chore.
With eyes that are always the same, they look at me. The vibe isn't that of just a bartender; it’s something that can’t be hidden.
Those eyes seem to suggest they have sharp claws and fangs hidden beneath a calm expression, giving off a samurai vibe.
Am I praising too much?
It feels like they've been standing there since birth, completely blending into the atmosphere of the bar.
With white skin illuminated by the purple-red light, their figure emerges from the dimness with an indescribable intensity.
Oh, what happened to your hand?
Ah.
It's nothing.
I've heard that even a small injury can be life-threatening, so please be careful.
Thank you.
If someone becomes mentally ill, isn't it something that's not easily cured?
That's true. It's possible to relapse over just a little trigger.
It’s definitely worse than physical ailments.
Yeah.
I get it, I understand that, but...
When you see it in person, you just don't know what to do.
You shouldn't be in such a state too. The people around you should be a support.
You seem to be getting down every time you come here lately.
Do I really look like a murderer?
Pfft, I was wondering what you were suddenly going to say!
How can that gentle face of yours look like a murderer?
Whether I have a gentle face or not aside, it's shocking to know that there are people who think I'm the culprit.
Who exactly do they say I killed?
Eva Courtney. A man who was her colleague said that to me.
Oh.
Speaking of which, her dismembered body was found, right? If I remember correctly, some parts of her body were missing.
If I’m told something I have no recollection of, it’s best to not worry about it.
You seem tired, but try not to think too deeply about it; time may solve some things.
The only thing I'm worried about is your health. It's better to take it easy and not stress too much.
I know. Besides, I don't feel stressed.
Even so, you seem to have accumulated some fatigue, so it might be best to take care of yourself.
I think it would be a good idea.
I'm glad you're coming here, though.
But please don't bring any impossible demands.
I'd prefer if you could avoid that.
Is the culprit the one behind the serial murder case?
Well, who knows?
By doing that, they might be camouflaging.
But there's still a possibility.
Do you have any grounds for that?
She came to my office before she was killed, bragging about having a scoop on the serial murder case.
And that leaves us with the question,
No, then her colleagues might have been killed by the serial murder case's perpetrator.
Don't you think so, normally?
I think so. So, maybe he wasn’t informed about that fact.
If that's the case, it raises the mystery of why Eva told you that story.
Right? Was there some meaning to it, or was it right after she became certain?
If there’s meaning, what kind could it be?
It’s quite a mystery.
Did you hear from Guy?
No, not at all.
I heard a rumor that Guy was thinking of putting an end to his player career.
I haven't heard any such thing.
Someone must have known that before consulting you, his best friend.
What do you mean?
His NETS contract should have expired last season, so the baseball team would need to negotiate a new one.
Oh, Mr. Garrison was talking about contract renewals, but we have to negotiate again. That's a hassle.
Is it okay to be this carefree? NETS disliked Guy's movie appearances, and he isn’t participating in the shoot.
That makes it sound like the baseball team is obstructing the filming.
I don’t really know the truth, but doesn't it seem suspicious?
I guess I’ll have to corner Garrison and get it out of him.
It seems worth a try.
I was curious about what you said, so I poked Garrison.
So, how did it go?
The result is, please never come back!
It was an attack.
It's definitely tied to the baseball team.
No way?
If we take it slow, they might slip up.
Still, what kind of scheme could be behind this? It's intriguing.
How's work going?
Uh.
Well, it's been quite rough.
If this job goes on forever, I won't need to find new work.
Oh no, I don’t want that.
If this goes on too long, it'll make my stomach sensitive.
That seems unlikely, doesn’t it?
They say haste makes waste.
So?
Why don't you just relax like you always do?
You should think about how being employed is already a blessing.
It looks like I'm about to run out of work.
Hey, don’t you have a knack for finding people?
You know Gabriela canceled on me, right? Plus, it seems like Meryl's situation is about to be resolved.
Shouldn't that normally be something to celebrate?
You know, I used to think detectives were amazing.
They can solve complex murder cases with minimal clues, right?
That's just a story in novels.
Yeah, but thinking about what I’m doing makes me feel pathetic.
Lately, you’ve been overthinking things.
I have no idea why I’m working at this bar or what kind of past I have.
He won't even talk about his past to me.
I guess I still haven't earned his trust.
I think I've talked too much about my secret?
If I'm being honest, a detective should keep secrets, and not tell anyone about a client's request.
Why does he support me? He must have no gain from this.
Does Andy really think I'm the culprit? I always thought he was a bit off.
I never expected him to be this simple-minded. I should probably avoid going to the editorial department's office for a while.
Why would the culprit do such a thing to Eva?
If it’s not Andy, then wouldn’t it be pointless to do something without a motive?
I feel sorry for Eva, but since I wasn't asked by anyone, I shouldn't get involved. I should leave it to the police.
Garrison's panic back then was definitely unusual. Could Guy be locked up by Nets?
Should I shake Garrison up a bit? If he confesses where Guy is, that would be great.
I just can't believe Guy would abandon Kristy and run off somewhere.
As long as Cindy agrees, Meryl's request will be fulfilled.
I understand Cindy's confusion, but if she could somehow agree to meet her mother...
There's no doubt that the matter with Meryl and Cindy is mostly resolved now.
What else is there to do?
It's not work, but can I really just leave the Ginny situation as it is?
Is finding Guy really my job? I remember being asked, but what about the reward? It can't be Kristy's body.
Only Meryl has officially accepted the request, but
Guy has to search, and Andy sees me as the culprit.
*** It's a bug ***
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
